en.extract:
The Uluguru mountain greenbul (Arizelocichla neumanni), formerly sometimes known as the Uluguru greenbul, is a species of the bulbul family of passerine birds.  It is found in eastern Tanzania.


Taxonomy and systematics
The Uluguru mountain greenbul was originally described in the genus Arizelocichla in 1922. 
It was then classified to the genus Andropadus before being re-classified back to the genus Arizelocichla in 2010. It was considered conspecific with the mountain greenbul until split to form a separate species in 2009. Some authorities have considered the Uluguru mountain greenbul to be a subspecies of the western mountain greenbul, mountain greenbul or black-browed mountain greenbul. Alternate names for the Uluguru mountain greenbul include the Mulanji mountain greenbul and Uluguru greenbul.

The '''Uluguru mountain greenbul''' ('''''Arizelocichla neumanni'''''), formerly sometimes known as the '''Uluguru greenbul''', is a species of the [[bulbul]] family of [[passerine]] [[bird]]s.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|title=Bulbuls « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-04-04}}</ref>  It is found in eastern Tanzania.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The Uluguru mountain greenbul was originally described in the genus ''[[Arizelocichla]]'' in 1922.<ref>

{{Cite journal|journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club|volume=42|issue=264|page=50|date=14 December 1921|title=Arizelocichla neumanni, p. nov.|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/item/100930#page/272/mode/1up |issn=0007-1595|access-date=2020-12-28}}</ref> 

It was then classified to the genus ''[[Andropadus]]'' before being re-classified back to the genus ''Arizelocichla'' in 2010.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archives/taxonomy-version-2/|title=Taxonomy Version 2 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-04-02}}</ref> It was considered conspecific with the [[mountain greenbul]] until split to form a separate species in 2009.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archives/species-2-0-2-11/|title=Species Version 2 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-04-04}}</ref> Some authorities have considered the Uluguru mountain greenbul to be a subspecies of the [[western mountain greenbul]], mountain greenbul or [[black-browed mountain greenbul]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=8EDD570A1B38983F|title=Arizelocichla neumanni - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2017-04-05}}</ref> Alternate names for the Uluguru mountain greenbul include the '''Mulanji mountain greenbul''' and '''Uluguru greenbul'''.
